title: Describing what's happening now
objectives: Form the present continuous with am/is/are + verb-ing
Use it for actions happening right now
Tell the difference between simple present and present continuous
intro: The present continuous describes actions happening at this very moment. Let's see how it works and when to use it.

concept.title: Am / is / are + verb-ing
concept.explanation:
We form the present continuous with the verb to be (am/is/are) plus a verb ending in -ing.

The structure is:
Subject + am/is/are + verb-ing (+ object).
examples: She is talking to her teacher.
They are playing in the garden.
concept.note: Right-now signal words include now, right now, at the moment, and look! / listen!

questions.feedback: is + crying → "The baby is crying" describes an action happening now.

questions.prompt: What is the structure of "She is reading a book"?

concept.title: Now vs. in general
concept.explanation:
Use the present continuous for what is happening now, and the simple present for habits and general truths.

Compare:
I am drinking tea now. (this moment)
I drink tea every morning. (habit)
concept.note: Some verbs (like, know, want, need) are usually NOT used in the continuous form.

questions.prompt: "I am talking to my teacher." This sentence describes an action that is...
questions.hint: The present continuous points to this moment.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence for a habit.
options: She is going to school every day.
She goes to school every day.
She go to school every day.
She going to school every day.
questions.hint: "Every day" is a habit, so use the simple present.
questions.feedback: Habits use the simple present: She goes to school every day.

assignment.title: Final check
assignment.intro: A short review and a reflection to finish.

questions.feedback: we are learning — an action happening at this moment.

questions.prompt: Write 2–3 sentences about what is happening around you right now.
